macro_summary: Markets enter a holiday-shortened week with June NFP data releasing this morning (8:30 AM ET) against a softening ADP print (98K vs. ~115K consensus), while the Fed's higher-for-longer posture keeps rate-hike fears alive into the July 28-29 FOMC meeting. Mega-cap tech earnings cluster July 28-30 alongside a potential tariff cliff on July 24, creating a stacked binary risk environment for an S&P 500 hovering near all-time highs. AI infrastructure spend remains the dominant equity theme, with SK Hynix's historic Nasdaq debut on ~July 10 and NVIDIA/Microsoft earnings in late July/August as the key single-stock catalysts. VIX near 16 keeps defined-risk options structures relatively affordable across the board.
screening_note: 9 overnight screening runs analyzed; minimum threshold of 2 appearances required for inclusion. 6 tickers qualified. MSFT and SKHY achieved perfect 9/9 consensus.
key_events: July 2 (today): June Nonfarm Payrolls, 8:30 AM ET — first major macro print into holiday weekend
July 7: Markets reopen with full repricing of NFP data
~July 10: SK Hynix (SKHY) Nasdaq ADR trading debut — largest ADR listing in history (~$29.4B)
July 24: 10% blanket Section 122 tariff expiry or extension decision
July 28 (after close): Microsoft Q4 FY2026 earnings — coincides with FOMC decision day
July 30 (after close): Apple Q3 FY2026 earnings
August 26 (after close): NVIDIA Q2 FY2027 earnings

ideas.name: Microsoft Corporation
ideas.thesis: MSFT appeared in all 9 overnight screens with 7 high-conviction votes, making it the top consensus idea. The stock has reset to ~22x earnings (from 30x) despite an AI annual run rate exceeding $37B (up 123% YoY) and Azure guided to 39-40% CC growth in Q4 FY2026 — creating a rare valuation dislocation vs. a confirmed July 28 earnings catalyst. Trading ~$130 below the 97-analyst consensus price target of $562 (33% upside gap), a defined-risk August bull call spread is the preferred structure, particularly given FOMC decision noise on the same day.
ideas.catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings confirmed July 28, 2026 after market close — same day as FOMC rate decision
ideas.edge: P/E compressed to 22x (from 30x) vs. 33% upside to $562 consensus PT; Azure guided 39-40% CC growth; prior two quarters beat EPS by +5.18-5.43% and revenue by +1.98%; FY2027 EPS consensus $19.09 supports continued re-rating
ideas.bull_case: Azure beats 39-40% CC growth guide and AI run-rate commentary drives multiple re-expansion toward analyst consensus PT of $562; buyback and CapEx conviction (>$40B Q4) reinforces long-duration AI infrastructure narrative.
ideas.bear_case: FOMC surprise rate hike on same day (July 28) compresses tech multiples across the board; any Azure deceleration signal below 39% would erase the core bull thesis rapidly.

ideas.name: SK Hynix (Nasdaq ADR)
ideas.thesis: SKHY appeared in all 9 overnight screens with the highest conviction vote total (8 high, 1 medium), making it the joint top consensus idea. The world's dominant HBM memory supplier (~57% global HBM market share, 72% Q1 2026 operating margin exceeding NVIDIA's 65%) debuts on Nasdaq around July 10 at a single-digit forward P/E versus NVIDIA at 40x+ — a pure valuation unlock driven by removal of the 'Korea Discount.' HSBC projects a 20% valuation re-rating at listing (P/B target raised from 2.8x to 3.4x, ADS target ~$166). As the largest ADR listing in history at ~$29.4B, options are unlikely to be available at launch, so stock entry with disciplined sizing is the primary vehicle.
ideas.catalyst: Nasdaq ADR trading debut tentatively July 10, 2026 — largest ADR listing in history at ~$29.4B; Korea Discount removal is the day-one re-rating trigger
ideas.edge: HSBC 20% valuation uplift forecast; single-digit forward P/E vs. NVDA at 40x+; 57% global HBM market share; 72% operating margin exceeds NVIDIA's 65%; ADS projected near $166; no prior US float means trapped institutional demand unlocked at listing
ideas.bull_case: Listing-day demand from US institutional investors who previously lacked access drives immediate re-rating toward HSBC's 20% uplift target; sustained HBM cycle tailwind (NVDA/hyperscaler CapEx) supports multi-quarter earnings visibility.
ideas.bear_case: IPO-day volatility on the largest ADR listing in history could cause significant price dislocation in both directions; memory cycle downturn risk and geopolitical Korea-US trade tensions could cap the Korea Discount removal thesis.

ideas.name: NVIDIA Corporation
ideas.thesis: NVDA appeared in 7 of 9 overnight screens, pulled back ~2% in the July 1 broad semiconductor selloff despite being a hyperscaler beneficiary with no company-specific negative news — creating an entry gap into the August 26 Q2 FY2027 earnings print. Q2 revenue is already guided to $91B against a 62-analyst consensus price target of $298.93 vs. ~$200 current price (49% implied upside). A defined-risk call spread (August or September expiry) captures the 8-week runway to earnings with capped downside if macro/AI-bubble fears persist; the MGX $49B AI fund announcement (July 1) signals sustained institutional demand for the infrastructure cycle.
ideas.catalyst: Q2 FY2027 earnings confirmed August 26, 2026 after market close; Q2 revenue already guided to $91B
ideas.edge: 49% gap to 62-analyst consensus PT of $298.93; Q1 FY2027 beat EPS by +5.65% and revenue by +3.04%; Data Center revenue $75.2B (+92% YoY); gross margin 74.1%; selloff on July 1 was sector-driven, not company-specific — creating a reset entry
ideas.bull_case: Q2 print confirms $91B guide or beats, reigniting AI infrastructure narrative and closing the gap to consensus PT; MGX $49B fund and hyperscaler CapEx commitments provide forward earnings visibility that re-rates the stock.
ideas.bear_case: A hawkish FOMC on July 29 or AI-bubble sentiment shift could keep valuation multiple compressed even on a fundamental beat; export control escalation or China demand weakness could impair Data Center growth trajectory.

ideas.name: Apple Inc.
ideas.thesis: AAPL appeared in 5 of 9 overnight screens into a confirmed July 30 Q3 FY2026 earnings print, with a consistent two-quarter beat cadence (+4.15% EPS, +2.09% revenue last quarter, stock up 3.69% on that print) and a $100B buyback providing a valuation floor. Stock is trading ~6% off its 52-week high of $317.40 (~$298 current), offering a reset entry ahead of a $108.86B consensus revenue quarter. A bull call spread into earnings suits the moderate-risk profile better than outright shares given macro/rate uncertainty.
ideas.catalyst: Q3 FY2026 earnings confirmed July 30, 2026 after market close; consensus revenue forecast $108.86B
ideas.edge: Last quarter beat EPS by +4.15% and revenue by +2.09% with stock +3.69% post-print; $100B buyback authorized; 49.3% gross margin on record $29.6B net income; stock ~6% below 52-week high creating a reset entry
ideas.bull_case: Back-to-back beat cadence extends with Apple Intelligence AI integration driving Services and hardware upsell momentum; $100B buyback and record margins reinforce the quality floor narrative.
ideas.bear_case: Consumer spending softness in a higher-for-longer rate environment could disappoint hardware unit volumes; July 30 print lands two days after a potentially hawkish FOMC, compressing the multiple before results even process.

ideas.name: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust
ideas.thesis: SPY appeared in 3 of 9 overnight screens exclusively as a defined-risk hedge idea, with zero high-conviction votes — reflecting broad agreement on downside tail risk rather than directional enthusiasm. A soft NFP print today into a 3-day holiday weekend creates a repricing gap risk on July 7 reopening, followed by the July 24 tariff cliff and July 28-29 FOMC as stacked binary catalysts against an S&P near all-time highs (~7,478). ADP came in at 98K vs. ~115K consensus, and Fed futures already price 3.8% funds rate by year-end. Low VIX (~16) keeps put spread premiums contained, making this a cost-efficient portfolio hedge for the concentrated watchlist above.
ideas.catalyst: June NFP today (8:30 AM ET); July 7 market reopen after holiday gap; July 24 tariff expiry; July 28-29 FOMC rate decision
ideas.edge: VIX near 16.15 keeps put spread premiums relatively cheap; ADP miss (98K vs. ~115K) raises NFP downside risk; rate-hike probability still at 27.3% for July FOMC despite recent Warsh dovish comments; defined-risk structure appropriate for moderate-risk profile
ideas.bull_case: NFP comes in soft, markets interpret as rate-cut catalyst rather than growth scare; Warsh FOMC delivers a hold with dovish tone, resolving the macro overhang and sending S&P to JPMorgan's 7,800 target.
ideas.bear_case: Hawkish FOMC surprise on July 29 (or hot tariff extension) hits tech multiples hard into a week where MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, and META all report — put spread pays off as a hedge on the concentrated long book above.

ideas.name: Invesco QQQ Trust
ideas.thesis: QQQ appeared in 2 of 9 overnight screens at the minimum qualifying threshold, viewed from two angles: a put spread hedge against a hawkish FOMC crushing tech multiples, and a call spread for those positioned for a soft-landing hold outcome. The mega-cap earnings cluster (MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, META all reporting July 28-30) coinciding with FOMC creates a binary vol event for tech-heavy Nasdaq. Low VIX (16.15) makes both structures relatively affordable. This is the weakest consensus idea and should be sized accordingly — it functions best as a complement to SPY hedging or as a defined-risk expression of the soft-landing bull case.
ideas.catalyst: FOMC rate decision July 28-29; mega-cap tech earnings cluster July 28-30 (MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, META)
ideas.edge: VIX at 16.15 keeps options affordable; Nasdaq fell 0.8% into holiday on chip drag despite soft jobs data; JPMorgan raised S&P 2026 target to 7,800 (+5% upside); Fed futures pricing ~3.8% by September creates hawkish surprise risk unpriced in QQQ near ATH
ideas.bull_case: FOMC holds rates with a dovish lean, mega-cap earnings beat broadly, and Nasdaq re-rates through the earnings cluster toward JPMorgan's index target — August call spread captures leveraged upside.
ideas.bear_case: Hawkish Warsh surprise on July 29 compresses AI/growth multiples into the earnings cluster, invalidating beats due to multiple contraction — put spread or no position preferred in that scenario.
